title: Service Blueprint IR
description: Intermediate representation for a service blueprint, authored as human-editable YAML in the workspace blueprint/ directory and validated by scripts/validate_ir.py. Mirrors the template data model (see references/data-model.md): lifecycle -> phases -> scenarios -> paths -> layers/steps/cells/triggers. Every entity carries a stable, locale-independent string key; database UUIDs are UUIDv5-derived from the key path (lifecycle/phase/scenario/path/layer/step) plus the locale, NFC-normalized, so re-imports are idempotent by construction. Cells are identified by their (path, layer, step) key triple; triggers by their source->target cell pair. Renaming a key after import creates a NEW identity on the next import. ALL human-readable text fields are locale maps ({en: ..., zh: ...}; additional locales allowed); structural fields (keys, enums, orders, URLs) are locale-independent.

schema_version.description: Template schema version this IR was authored against. Import performs a compatibility check against the workspace clone's schema_version (see references/workspace-state.md).

locales.description: Locales this blueprint is authored in. Every localeText field should cover all declared locales; the validator flags gaps. One generated artifact set and one deploy target per locale.

key.description: Stable identifier: lowercase alphanumerics, hyphen, underscore. Locale-independent. Feeds UUIDv5 derivation — treat as permanent once imported.

locale.description: BCP-47-style locale tag, e.g. en, zh, zh-Hant.

localeText.description: Locale -> text map, e.g. {en: 'Submit repair ticket', zh: '提交报修工单'}.

source.description: Source document path or URL this content was extracted from.

section.description: Section heading, page, sheet, or frame within the source.

cellRef.description: Reference to a cell on the same path by its (layer, step) key pair.

type.description: url = external link chip; tech_description = long-form copy attached to a tech pill, keyed by the pill label.

picture.description: Screenshot/illustration path or URL for tech_description links.

pictures.description: Multiple images (e.g. logo + screenshot); takes precedence over picture.

loops_to.description: Key of another phase this phase loops back to (loops_to_phase_id).

view_type.description: single = one path at a time; side-by-side = labeled variant comparison (see paths[].variant_label); integrated = runtime merge of all paths.

steps.description: Scenario-scoped step columns, shared across paths. Per-path column order lives in paths[].path_steps.

note.description: Optional note shown with path metadata (e.g. parallel-scenario context).

variant_label.description: Label for this path as a comparison variant in side-by-side view (e.g. 'as designed' vs 'as actually done'). Any two labeled variants compare — designed-vs-reality is just the default labeling.

path_steps.description: Scenario step keys in column order for THIS path (array index = column_position). Every cell's step must appear here — the DB trigger aborts imports otherwise.

display_name.description: Free-form swimlane label in any language (layers.name in the DB).

role.description: Semantic role key (layers.layer_role) driving rendering and divider lines. Canonical: customer_actions, frontstage_actions, backstage_actions, frontstage_tech, backstage_tech, support_systems, visual, step_visual. Org-defined custom roles and null both render as generic swimlanes. See references/layer-roles.md.

content.description: Cell label, the primary grid text. Newline-separated items render as pills on pill-role lanes. Omit (or empty strings) for visual-row picture cells.

picture.description: Image path or URL (visual rows and illustrated cells).

needs_review.description: Low-confidence extraction — surface during review; sign-off should clear or confirm these.

evidence.description: Verbatim quotes or pointers backing this cell (researcher traceability). Reserved shape; no v1 UX.

attribution.description: Who asserted this content (stakeholder-merge conflicts modeled as data). Reserved shape; no v1 UX.

trigger.description: Directed trigger arrow between two cells on the SAME path (cross-path triggers are invalid). source != target.
